page: Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

The x86_64 architecture of this instance includes advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate matrix multiplication and vector operations. It also features hardware-assisted cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, alongside a large 480 MB L3 cache that supports memory-intensive operations.

Benchmark results place the r8i-flex.4xlarge in the top 10% tier for text processing with a score of 2930.0 in Geekbench and small model LLM text generation at 361.46 tokens per second. Single-threaded operations show strong performance, with Geekbench single-core scoring 2161.0 and gzip compression reaching 34.04 GB/sec. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations sit within the average range.

The lack of local NVMe SSD storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. However, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and DDR5 memory speed make this instance suitable for memory-bound applications, text processing pipelines, small-scale language model inference, and database caching layers.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8i-flex.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmark evaluations, it demonstrates top-tier performance in text processing and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong single-core processing. Conversely, its multi-core metrics, database operations, and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier. Lacking local storage, it relies on network storage, making it suitable for memory-intensive applications, text processing, and caching databases.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive workloads and text processing.

128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
